relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Do not place yourself over others as final judge.
The measure you use becomes the measure you live inside.
It is easy to see another’s speck while missing the beam in yourself.
Unclear sight cannot gently guide another’s sight.
Clear your own eye first, then help becomes true.
Discernment protects what is holy from being wasted or trampled.
Ask, seek, and knock; the path opens through living trust.
The seeking heart is not ignored.
A true parent does not answer hunger with stone.
Nor does a true parent answer need with harm.
Even mixed humans know how to give good gifts; the Father gives more deeply.
Treat others as you desire to be treated; this gathers Torah and Prophets into lived love.
The gate into life is narrow, not because love is small, but because illusion is wide.
The compressed way forms the soul toward life.
Discern teachers by what lives beneath their covering.
Fruit reveals the root.
A good tree bears life-giving fruit; a troubled tree bears troubled fruit.
Fruit cannot be faked forever.
Fruitless patterns cannot remain in the refining fire of truth.
Recognition comes through fruit.
Calling the name is not the same as doing the Father’s will.
Even impressive works can miss true knowing.
Work without right alignment is not intimacy with the teacher.
Hearing and doing build the house on rock.
Storms come, but rock-founded life stands.
Hearing without doing builds on sand.
The same storm reveals the foundation.
The crowds are astonished when the teaching ends.
Jesus teaches with authority that feels different from ordinary scribal instruction.
